common.description: Barebone setup with Next 16, Zustand, Tailwind, and App Router. No MUI.

splash.title: Just a moment
splash.subtitle: Please wait while the app finishes this step.

hero.descriptionLine1: A developer boilerplate designed to disappear into your workflow.
hero.descriptionLine2: Clean structures, quiet operations, premium defaults.

philosophy.introP1: This boilerplate was founded on a simple premise: developer templates shouldn't feel noisy. They should feel like a smooth stone or a clear window—providing structural utility when you work, and remaining invisible when you compile.
philosophy.introP2: In an age of infinite dependency bloat, we choose only the essential tools. We use pure standard Web APIs, vanilla layouts, and native configs, establishing a serene connection between your development flow and browser execution.
philosophy.block1Body: We reject the disposable dependency packages. Every utility is crafted using standard React, Next.js, and browser-native APIs to ensure codebases that remain maintainable, stable, and highly performant over time.
philosophy.block2Body: Our structures respect your resources. No background telemetry trackers, no heavy third-party UI framework bloat. Just calm utility when you build, and lightweight clean bundles when you deploy.

j1.title: The Philosophy of Zero Bloat
j1.excerpt: Why modern codebases crave lightweight, native browser solutions instead of heavy UI frameworks.
j1.content: We live in an era of massive frameworks. A simple landing page pulls down megabytes of dependencies, slowing page load times and complicating maintenance. True codebase quality starts by using standard APIs and clean architecture. Reclaiming sensory simplicity in development means designing code that respects CPU and bundle limits alike.
j1.imageAlt: Journal cover: The Philosophy of Zero Bloat

j2.title: Securing Modern Web Apps
j2.excerpt: A deep dive into site-wide symmetric payload encryption using standard Web Crypto APIs.
j2.content: Security shouldn't add bloat. Native browser SubtleCrypto APIs allow developers to secure all client-server communication using AES-256-GCM. By wrapping standard API route handlers dynamically, payloads are secure, performance is maintained, and external library bloat remains at absolute zero.
j2.imageAlt: Journal cover: Securing Modern Web Apps

j3.title: Circadian Coding Practices
j3.excerpt: Notes on creating serene workspaces, zero background noise, and calm dev workflows.
j3.content: In an age of endless context switching, focus is the ultimate premium resource. Circadian coding is the practice of aligning development workflows with focus cycles. Quiet workspaces, muted color palettes, clean logs, and minimal dependency noise are essential to sustain long-term coding health.

footer.brandBody: Designing code structures that feel as clean and natural as the physical world. Born for speed, built for the mind.

f1.name: Next.js 16 App Router
f1.description: Built on Next.js 16 with Turbopack compilation. Includes custom-crafted layouts, parallel routing, and API route proxying.
f1.longDescription: Experience navigation as it was meant to be - unconfined and lightning fast. This template leverages Next.js 16 App Router conventions and native folder structures. Encased in Turbopack compilation, pages render dynamically with optimized asset delivery.

f2.name: Zustand Global Store
f2.description: Reactive state management that tracks authentication, theme states, and network request logging with minimal boilerplate.
f2.longDescription: State shouldn't be a sequence of complex boilerplate. The Zustand store offers clean global hooks for user auth sessions, circadian theme switches, and developer tools, ensuring your code remains extremely readable.

f3.name: Tailwind CSS & Theme
f3.description: Circadian light & dark modes styled with soft sandstone and warm charcoal tones. Styled cleanly with Tailwind CSS.
f3.longDescription: Aesthetics shouldn't feel like looking into a lightbulb. Re-styled with a matte nano-sandstone texture and candle-like warm dark mode, the UI respects your attention and is fully responsive.

f4.name: AES-256-GCM Encryption
f4.description: Site-wide cryptographic wrapping for API payloads using standard SubtleCrypto Web APIs. Protects your communication.
f4.longDescription: Security should be default. By implementing client-to-server payload wrapping using symmetric AES-256-GCM, your API inputs and outputs remain completely secure in transit with zero external overhead.

f5.name: Vitest & Playwright Tests
f5.description: Robust testing architecture containing unit, integration, and E2E browser flows to safeguard deployment stability.
f5.longDescription: Never push broken features. Configured with a synchronous translation mock for Vitest unit testing, and dynamic session flows for Playwright E2E browser tests, testing becomes a natural part of development.

f6.name: cURL Interceptor DevTool
f6.description: Interactive network logger capturing axios queries and formatting request data as clean shell-escaped cURL scripts.
f6.longDescription: The connection between development and debugging should be seamless. The built-in devtool intercepts outgoing queries, formats header variables, and exposes copying actions directly on the UI overlay.
